# Contract: Cross-Source De-Duplication FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `013-cross-source-dedup` | **Date**: 2026-07-19
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + all-reader + balance-chain + reconciliation `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external
interface" is the exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the
consumer-facing form. This slice adds **one function** (`cross_source_duplicates`) and **two new types**
(`DedupLayer` enum + `CrossSourceMatch` record). It **reuses** the existing `Transaction`/`Direction`
records/enums and the `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types **unchanged**, and requires **no `uniffi.toml`
change**. Mirrors the `reconcile_statement`-wraps-`reconcile` precedent exactly.

---

## Exported function

### `cross_source_duplicates`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn cross_source_duplicates(existing: Vec<Transaction>, incoming: Vec<Transaction>) -> Vec<CrossSourceMatch>` |
| Swift | `public func crossSourceDuplicates(existing: [Transaction], incoming: [Transaction]) -> [CrossSourceMatch]` |

**Contract**

- Pure batch matcher over **two already-parsed** transaction lists (produced by any `read_*_statement` +
  the app's own `Transaction` construction). **Never** opens a file/PDF, never touches the
  network/clock/locale/global state, never queries a database (FR-005/014/016).
- **Two-layer ladder per incoming row** (canonical **before** fuzzy; first **unconsumed** existing wins):
  - **Canonical** — same `date`, same `amount` (exact `Decimal` value, `250.00` == `250.0`), same
    `direction`, and equal **first-60-char** normalised-narration prefix → `layer == .canonical`.
  - **Fuzzy** (only if no canonical hit) — same `amount`, same `direction`, dates **within ±1 day**, and
    Jaro-Winkler similarity **≥ 0.92** (inclusive) on the normalised narrations → `layer == .fuzzy`.
- **Multiplicity-aware**: each existing row is consumed by **at most one** incoming row; surplus incoming
  repeats survive (FR-003). **Read-only**: neither input list is mutated, dropped, reordered, merged, or
  persisted (FR-002).
- **Result**: `[CrossSourceMatch]` in incoming order — each names the matched incoming row
  (`incomingIndex`), the existing row it duplicates (`existingIndex`), and the `layer`. An incoming row
  with no match is a **survivor** and is **absent** from the result. Empty input (either side) ⇒ `[]`.
- **Purity / totality**: identical input ⇒ identical output; never panics; no failure is surfaced to Swift
  (total function) (FR-005, SC-012). A Jaro-Winkler similarity is `f64` internally; **no money is ever a
  float** — amounts are exact `Decimal` (FR-011).

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**

- Treat the result as the set of identified duplicates; **survivors are the incoming indices not present**
  in any match. Do **not** infer deletion/merge — this call only **identifies** (acting on duplicates is a
  later slice) (FR-002, US5).
- Use `layer` to distinguish a clean canonical duplicate (`.canonical`) from a near-duplicate caught by
  similarity/±1-day skew (`.fuzzy`).
- Dereference `existingIndex`/`incomingIndex` into the same `[Transaction]` arrays you passed in (the
  indices are positional and stable for that call).

> Internally: the exported `cross_source_duplicates` wraps `crate::dedup::cross_source_duplicates(&existing,
> &incoming)` (borrowed slices), exactly as `reconcile_statement` wraps `reconcile(&statement)`. `lib.rs`
> re-exports the **FFI** function (`pub use ffi::cross_source_duplicates;`) and the dedup types + narration
> helper (`pub use dedup::{CrossSourceMatch, DedupLayer, normalize_narration};`). The pure
> `dedup::cross_source_duplicates` is **not** re-exported at the crate root (name clash) — `tests/parity.rs`
> uses the FFI-exported one via `kaname_core::cross_source_duplicates`.

---

## Types

### `DedupLayer` (NEW) — `uniffi::Enum`

| Rust | Swift |
|-|-|
| `enum DedupLayer { Canonical, Fuzzy }` | `.canonical` / `.fuzzy` |

Two variants. Mirrors `ChainStatus`/`ReconcileStatus` derives
(`Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/uniffi::Enum`).

### `CrossSourceMatch` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record`

Swift shape (generated):

```swift
public struct CrossSourceMatch {
    public var incomingIndex: UInt32   // position in the `incoming` array
    public var existingIndex: UInt32   // position in the `existing` array (consumed once)
    public var layer: DedupLayer       // .canonical or .fuzzy
}
```

Mirrors `ChainResult`/`ReconcileResult` (`Debug/Clone/PartialEq/uniffi::Record`). Rows are named **by
index** (`Transaction` has no id; D8).

### `Transaction` / `Direction` (REUSED — unchanged)

- `record Transaction { date: String /* ISO-8601 */; description: String; amount: Decimal; direction: Direction }`
  — the same record P1+readers already expose. `amount` crosses as a `Decimal` custom type
  (`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal`); `date` as an ISO-8601 `String`. **No field change.**
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → `.debit` / `.credit`. Compared for equality by the matcher; never
  re-derived from the amount's sign.
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String` (P1, `ffi.rs` +
  `uniffi.toml`) are reused. Money never crosses as `f64`. **No `uniffi.toml` change.**

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-022/024)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `dedup.rs` unit tests):

- `cross_source_dedup_matches_expected` (parity): load `fixtures/dedup/cross_source/basic.json`, build
  `existing`/`incoming` via `Transaction::new`, call `cross_source_duplicates`, and assert the returned
  `Vec<CrossSourceMatch>` equals `expected_matches` (`{0,0,Canonical}`, `{1,1,Fuzzy}`, `{4,4,Canonical}`)
  — incoming_index / existing_index / layer (SC-013/014).
- `dedup.rs` unit tests additionally cover the normaliser references, the Jaro-Winkler reference values
  (4-dp), and canonical / fuzzy-0.92-boundary / below-threshold / direction+amount+date guards /
  multiplicity / precedence / determinism (SC-001..SC-012).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`, `ios/Tests/CrossSourceDedupTests.swift`)** — "core ↔ Swift
cross-source dedup" (mirrors `ReconcileTests`):

- Build `[Transaction]` `existing` + `incoming` with a clean canonical duplicate → `crossSourceDuplicates`
  returns a match with `layer == .canonical` and the right `incomingIndex`/`existingIndex`.
- A near-duplicate (≥ 0.92, ±1 day) → a match with `layer == .fuzzy`.
- Two identical incoming vs one existing → **exactly one** match; the surplus incoming index is **absent**
  (survivor) (SC-005/014).
- `DedupLayer` surfaces as `.canonical`/`.fuzzy`; `CrossSourceMatch` as
  `incomingIndex`/`existingIndex`/`layer`. `Decimal(string:locale: en_US_POSIX)` for amounts. Any comment
  sits on its **own line above** the code (swift-format `[Spacing]` forbids trailing inline `//`).

---

## Stability / compatibility

- Adding one function + two types is additive; no existing signature changes, and `Transaction`/`Direction`
  are reused unchanged, so existing callers, fixtures, and the generated Swift for prior slices are
  unaffected.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts, rebuilt by
  `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not the generated
  files, is the reviewed source of truth.
